summary: This post is a practical DIY tutorial for making cloth face masks during the early COVID-19 pandemic. O'Neil provides a step-by-step sewing guide using dishcloths, wire for nose fitting, and elastic cord. She references an existing pattern but aims to simplify the instructions for accessibility. The post includes material recommendations with links, measurements, and photos of each step. It's a departure from her usual algorithmic criticism, reflecting the urgent community response to PPE shortages in spring 2020.
key_points: Thicker cotton materials like dishcloths provide better filtration for DIY masks
A cardboard template allows for efficient batch production of multiple masks
The design includes a wire channel for nose fitting to improve seal
The construction uses an inside-out turning technique common in sewn items
Elastic cord threaded through flaps creates adjustable ear loops
money_quotes: But to be honest I found it hard to follow which is why I'm going to tell you quite plainly how to do this relatively quickly.
This has been used a bunch and is kind of a mess. That's ok.
I got 12 cardboard outlines on! That means 3 masks.
key_insight: During a crisis, even data scientists turn to direct action—sometimes the most useful thing you can do is make masks and share clear instructions.
